explanations.3: The parenthesis here creates a subshell, which is a separate environment that won't affect your current shell's variables when it exits.
explanations.4: The && operator means the next command only runs if this one succeeds; it's a way to chain commands safely.
explanations.5: The $(pwd) and $(id -u):$(id -g) are command substitution—they run those commands and replace them with their output so docker knows your current directory and user permissions.
